oneOf.description: IRI of the entire resource being annotated

target.description: W3C Web Annotation target - can be a simple IRI string (entire resource) or an object with source and optional selector (fragment)

oneOf.description: Non-empty array of mixed TextualBody (tagging) and SpecificResource (linking) bodies

body.description: W3C Web Annotation body. Optional per the W3C spec — annotations whose motivation alone is meaningful (highlighting) legitimately omit it. Present values are either a single body or a non-empty array of bodies; the prior empty-array 'stub' branch has been removed (it was a naming lie shared between highlights and never-actually-emitted stub references, and the source of the #651 reference-annotation validator bug).

creator.description: Web Annotation creator — the entity that initiated the annotation. For human-driven work this is a Person; for autonomous-agent work this is a Software peer.

generator.description: Web Annotation generator — the SoftwareAgent that produced the annotation, when software was involved. Absent for purely manual annotations. Single object is the common case; array supports pipelines that combine multiple software peers.

wasAttributedTo.description: PROV-O wasAttributedTo — all parties responsible for this annotation. For human-prompted AI work this combines `creator` (the Person) and `generator` (the Software). For purely manual annotations it equals `[creator]`; for autonomous-agent work it equals `[generator]` (and `creator` may be the same Software).
